zh_CN: 迎狐之仪。\n供物入御供筒，不可手捧。\n祭坛从底向上读。后放入者，在上。
en: Welcoming the Fox ritual.\\nPlace offerings into the offering tube; do not carry them by hand.\\nRead the altar from bottom to top. The later offering goes on top.

zh_CN: 狐上山，不回头。\n若回头，莫唤名。\n人名入狐耳，山灯不长明。
en: Fox goes up the mountain and does not look back.\\nIf it does, do not call its name.\\nOnce a human name enters a fox's ears, the mountain lamps will not stay lit.

zh_CN:
装束供物三件。
杉木为底。
白毛覆身。
蓬草盖顶。
en: Three offerings for the costume.\\nSugi wood as the base.\\nWhite fur over the body.\\nMugwort on top.

zh_CN: ……送……本社……\n……白……覆身……\n……不得呼名……
en: ...send...the shrine...\\n...white...cover...\\n...do not speak the name...

zh_CN: 杉木很沉，砍口渗着冷水。旧规说，它是祭坛的底。
en: The Sugi wood is heavy, and cold water seeps from the cut. Old rules say it is the altar's base.

zh_CN: 蓬草味很浓，碰过的指尖都是苦的，似乎能压抑活物气息。
en: The mugwort is strongly scented. Your fingertips taste bitter after touching it, as if it can suppress living scent.

zh_CN: 箱里放着一束白色纤维，箱子上刻着「白毛」，边缘却压得很平，潮了也不会打卷。
en: A bundle of white fiber lies in the chest. The lid is marked 'white fur', and the edges are pressed flat, never curling even when damp.

zh_CN: 两样顶礼供物：旧铃铛和狐火石。
en: Two final offerings: the old bell and the foxfire stone.

zh_CN: 来了就好。昨晚山路又开始鬼打墙。
en: Good, you're here. Last night the mountain path started looping again.

zh_CN: 今晚上要照老规矩，把白狐迎回本社。这是御供筒
en: Tonight we follow the old rules and welcome the white fox back to the main shrine. This is the offering tube.

zh_CN: 老人把黑漆御供筒递给你。筒身冰冷，漆面沾着水，边上刻着细小的狐纹。
en: The elder hands you the black lacquer offering tube. It is cold, wet on the surface, with tiny fox patterns carved along the side.

zh_CN: 供物按顺序入筒。杉木、白毛、蓬草。
en: Put the offerings into the tube in order. Sugi wood, white fur, mugwort.

zh_CN: 若有什么不懂，按照山上的木牌做，千万别做错了。
en: If anything is unclear, follow the wooden signs on the mountain. Do not get it wrong.

zh_CN: 老人转过身，又记起来什么的样子，沙哑的声音悠悠传来，在山谷中回响
en: The elder turns away, then seems to remember something. Their hoarse voice drifts back and echoes through the valley.

zh_CN: 狐上山，不回头。若回头，莫唤名。
en: Fox goes up the mountain, and does not look back. If it does, do not call its name.

zh_CN: 什么名字？
en: What name?

zh_CN: 想不起来，就不要想。守规矩的人，活得久些。去吧。
en: If you can't remember, then don't think about it. Those who keep the rules live longer. Go.

zh_CN: 御供筒被放上祭坛。杉木、白色纤维和蓬草按顺序落定。
en: The offering tube is set on the altar. Sugi wood, white fiber, and mugwort settle in order.

zh_CN: 白色纤维摊开时，盖住了中间那层影子。
en: When the white fiber spreads open, it covers the shadow in the middle.

zh_CN: 祠后的纸门轻响。门缝里没有风。
en: The paper door behind the shrine gives a soft sound. There is no wind in the gap.

zh_CN: 你把旧铃铛放在供物最上方。
en: You place the old bell on top of the offerings.

zh_CN: 铃声很远，从山上的雾里落下来。
en: The ringing is distant, falling from the mountain mist.

zh_CN: 白狐从鸟居后走出。它没有靠近，只看了你一眼。眼睛湿得发亮。
en: The white fox steps out from behind the torii gate. It does not come closer, only looks at you once. Its eyes shine wetly.

zh_CN: 它转身上山，尾端的狐火平稳地亮着。
en: It turns and heads up the mountain, the foxfire at the tail steady and bright.

zh_CN: 你把狐火石嵌进供物之间。
en: You set the foxfire stone between the offerings.

zh_CN: 火纹猛地亮起，供桌边缘冒出焦味。
en: The flame pattern flares bright, and a smell of char rises from the altar's edge.

zh_CN: 白狐从火光里走出。它跑得很急，像被什么东西催促前行。
en: The white fox emerges from the flames. It runs in a hurry, as if driven forward by something.

zh_CN: 它转身上山，影子被火拉得很长。
en: It turns back up the mountain, its shadow stretched long by the fire.

zh_CN: 旧鸟居后的山路在浓雾中亮了一下。
en: The mountain path behind the old torii flashes once in the thick mist.

zh_CN: 白狐没有回头。狐火在门后等你。
en: The white fox does not look back. The foxfire waits for you behind the gate.

zh_CN:
装束顺序。
杉木为台。
白□覆身。
蓬草盖顶。
en: Order of dressing.\\nSugi wood as the platform.\\nWhite □ over the body.\\nMugwort on top.

zh_CN: 参道断时，以杉木承之。\n狐若不前，以铃安之。\n火若不旺，以油续之。
en: When the path breaks, support it with Sugi wood.\\nIf the fox will not move, soothe it with a bell.\\nIf the fire is weak, feed it oil.

zh_CN: 铃声安魂，狐火收敛。\n火石催行，狐火暴起。\n无论安抚或催促，狐都必须到本社。
en: Bell sounds soothe the soul, and the foxfire settles.\\nFoxfire stones drive it forward, and the foxfire surges.\\nWhether calmed or pushed, the fox must reach the main shrine.

zh_CN: 你又取下一段杉木。竹筒变轻，断路减少。
en: You take another length of Sugi wood. The tube grows lighter, and the broken route shrinks.

zh_CN: 你又取下一段杉木。竹筒变轻，断路减少。
en: You take another length of Sugi wood. The tube grows lighter, and the broken route shrinks.

zh_CN: 箱里的白色纤维叠得很整齐。边缘有一排细小针孔，摸上去硬硬的。
en: The white fibers in the chest are stacked neatly. There is a row of tiny pinholes at the edge, and they feel stiff to the touch.

zh_CN: 高处的蓬草湿得发冷。揉开以后，苦味盖过了一切气味。
en: The mugwort high up is cold and damp. Crushed open, its bitterness overwhelms every other smell.

zh_CN: 清水草贴着掌心，凉得像井底的水，似乎能熄灭一切烈焰。，莫名躁动的心一瞬间静下来，
en: The clear-water grass rests against your palm, cool like water from the bottom of a well, as if it can extinguish any flame. Your restless heart grows still in an instant.

zh_CN: 断处太宽。要用杉木承住参道。
en: The break is too wide. Use Sugi wood to bear the approach.

zh_CN: 高台太远。要用杉木搭过去。
en: The ledge is too far. Use Sugi wood to bridge it.

zh_CN: 你铺下杉木桥。
en: You lay down a Sugi wood bridge.

zh_CN: 你把筒里的杉木取出，架在断崖上。木头被雨泡得沉甸甸的。
en: You take the Sugi wood from the tube and set it across the cliff. The rain-soaked wood feels heavy.

zh_CN: 路能过了，但御供筒也空了一格。
en: The path is passable now, but the offering tube is one slot emptier.

zh_CN: 看来供物不只可以祭坛上用，也能修这条迎狐归位的路。
en: It seems offerings are not only for the altar; they can also repair the path for bringing the fox back.

zh_CN: 你把杉木铺在第二处断裂的参道上。
en: You lay the Sugi wood over the second break in the approach.

zh_CN: 木头压住碎石，脚下的空缺暂时安静下来。
en: The wood pins down the rubble, and the gap under your feet goes quiet for now.

zh_CN: 参道连上了，可以抵达平台。远处本社的灯变成冷蓝色，照得雨雾发白。
en: The approach is connected; you can reach the platform. In the distance, the main shrine lamps turn cold blue, whitening the rain mist.

zh_CN: 杉木斜搭在石台边。你踩上去，木头在雾里闷响。
en: The Sugi wood leans against the stone ledge. When you step on it, the wood gives a dull thud in the mist.

zh_CN: 普通灯火太浊，取不出能用于仪式的灯芯油。
en: The ordinary flame is too muddy. You cannot draw lamp oil useful for the ritual.

zh_CN: 你从蓝火里取出一小瓶灯芯油。火苗矮下去，灯罩里只剩湿冷的烟。
en: You draw out a small bottle of lamp oil from the blue flame. The flame sinks, leaving only damp, cold smoke in the lamp shade.

zh_CN: 你拉动旧铃绳。绳子吸饱了潮气，铃声闷闷地落进山谷，凭空出现一段平台，清水草在平台上轻轻晃动
en: You pull the old bell rope. Soaked with moisture, it sends a muffled ring into the valley, and a platform appears out of nowhere. The clear-water grass sways lightly on it.

zh_CN: 第二座祭坛接住了供物。石面渗出水。
en: The second altar catches the offerings. Water seeps from the stone surface.

zh_CN: 本社的灯亮了一点，白狐的影子出现在参道尽头。
en: The main shrine's lamps brighten a little, and the white fox's shadow appears at the end of the approach.

zh_CN: 还差最后一道顶礼。清水草压火，灯芯油添火。
en: Only the final offering remains. Clear-water grass calms the flame; lamp oil feeds it.

zh_CN: 你把清水草覆在供物上。
en: You cover the offering with clear-water grass.

zh_CN: 蓝火低下去，白狐的脚步慢下来，身子蜷缩起来，恍惚间好似学步小童。
en: The blue fire lowers. The white fox's steps slow, and its body curls up, briefly like a toddler learning to walk.

zh_CN: 至少别再催它了。
en: At least stop driving it on.

zh_CN: 你把灯芯油倒进火里。
en: You pour the lamp oil into the flame.

zh_CN: 狐火猛地照亮两侧石像。白狐的影子被拉长，像是披着宽袖踽踽前行。
en: The foxfire suddenly lights up the stone statues on both sides. The white fox's shadow stretches long, as if it were trudging forward in wide sleeves.

zh_CN: 那影子……不像狐。
en: That shadow... doesn't look like a fox.

zh_CN: 迎狐之仪。\n供物入御供筒，不可手捧。\n祭坛从底向上读。后放入者，在上。
en: Welcoming the Fox ritual.\\nOfferings go into the offering tube; do not carry them by hand.\\nRead the altar from bottom to top. The later offering goes on top.

zh_CN: 狐上山，不回头。\n若回头，莫唤名。\n人名入狐耳，山灯不长明。
en: Fox goes up the mountain and does not look back.\\nIf it does, do not call its name.\\nA human name in a fox's ear keeps the mountain lamp from burning long.

zh_CN:
装束供物三件。
杉木为底。
白毛覆身。
蓬草盖顶。
en: Three offerings for the costume.\\nSugi wood as the base.\\nWhite fur over the body.\\nMugwort on top.

zh_CN: ……送……本社……\n……白……覆身……\n……不得呼名……
en: ...send...the shrine...\\n...white...cover...\\n...do not speak the name...

zh_CN: 本社到了。
en: You've arrived at the main shrine.

zh_CN: 这里没有倒塌。灯笼、回廊和纸门都摆得很整齐。
en: Nothing here has collapsed. Lanterns, corridors, and paper doors are all neatly in place.

zh_CN: 地上没有脚印，供桌也没有香灰，比山路还干净
en: There are no footprints on the ground, and no ash on the offering table. It's cleaner than the mountain path.

zh_CN: 总觉得会有人在里面生活
en: It feels like someone should be living inside.

zh_CN: 可安静的什么声音都没有。
en: But the silence gives away nothing.

zh_CN: 表面的金漆写着「迎狐之仪」。
en: The gold paint on the surface reads 'Welcoming the Fox Ritual'.

zh_CN: 陈旧的木匾被潮气浸润，侵蚀了金漆，用狐火靠近查看，泡软的金漆斑驳
en: The old wooden plaque is soaked with moisture, eroding the gold paint. When you bring foxfire close, the softened paint flakes unevenly.

zh_CN: 下面一层的金漆更加陈旧，你使劲擦却只能擦出「送*之仪」的字样。
en: The layer beneath is even older. You scrub hard, but can only reveal the characters 'Sending * Ritual'.

zh_CN: 迎狐是后来印上去的字，原来这个仪式是送行，为什么是送，要送什么？
en: 'Welcoming the Fox' was added later. So the ritual was originally a sending ritual. Why send, and what was being sent?

zh_CN: 大疫第三年，山路七日不通。井水发黑，死者二十七。村人不再入山。
en: In the third year of the plague, the mountain path was blocked for seven days. The well water turned black, and twenty-seven people died. The villagers no longer went into the mountains.

zh_CN: 社中择送行者一名。白衣覆身，于子时送至本社。
en: One sender was chosen from the shrine. Dressed in white, they were sent to the main shrine at midnight.

zh_CN: 是夜后，山雾散。道路复正。井水渐清。病者亦退热。
en: After that night, the mountain mist cleared. The road straightened. The well water cleared. The sick also broke their fevers.

zh_CN: 次年重修神社。村中改称此仪为「迎狐」。旧称自此不再记于木额。
en: The next year, the shrine was rebuilt. The village renamed the ritual 'Welcoming the Fox'. The old name was no longer recorded on the plaque.

zh_CN: 送行者……送的是人？为什么要送人进神社？
en: The sender... was a person? Why send a person into the shrine?

zh_CN: 白*一领。童身用。袖口束紧。
en: One white * robe. For a child's body. Cuffs tied tight.

zh_CN: 有人用纸遮住了这个字，粘得很死，无法强行撕开
en: Someone covered this character with paper, glued on so tightly you cannot tear it off by force.

zh_CN: 杉木为轿。白□覆身。蓬草盖顶，以遮人气。
en: Sugi wood as the palanquin. White □ over the body. Mugwort on top to hide human scent.

zh_CN: 送行后，狐神归位，再不得呼其名。
en: After the sending, the fox deity returns to its seat, and its name must never again be called.

zh_CN: 本次送行者：雨宫纱夜，九岁。山社巫女。签选。家不得辞。
en: Sender for this ritual: Amamiya Sayo, age nine. Shrine maiden. Chosen by lot. Family may not refuse.

zh_CN: 送行后，名册除名。村中不得呼其名。家人不得回望。
en: After the sending, the name is struck from the roster. The village must not speak it. Family must not look back.

zh_CN: 狐位者，生身入位，化狐守灯，镇山路与旧病。若至亲呼名，狐位松动；狐位一空，诸灾复归。
en: The fox-seat bearer enters the seat in body and becomes a fox to guard the lamps, calming mountain roads and old disease. If a close relative calls the name, the seat loosens; if the seat empties, all disasters return.

zh_CN: 雨宫纱夜。
en: Amamiya Sayo.

zh_CN: 为什么她也叫雨宫，这个名字为什么如此耳熟。
en: Why does she also carry the name Amamiya? Why does it sound so familiar?

zh_CN: 大脑剧烈的疼痛，有什么东西要记起来了，你反复咀嚼着这个名字
en: Your head throbs violently. Something is trying to surface, and you keep turning the name over and over.

zh_CN: 雨宫纱夜，这是姐姐的名字。
en: Amamiya Sayo. That's your older sister's name.

zh_CN: 旧字写送，新字写迎。
en: The old characters say send, the new ones say welcome.

zh_CN: 有人把仪式的方向改反了，原本的送行变成了迎神
en: Someone reversed the ritual's direction. The original sending became a welcoming rite.

zh_CN: 纸上写着白，后面的字被框住了。
en: The paper says white, and the character after it is boxed in.

zh_CN: 装束札也缺了同一处，像是有人故意留下一个空格。
en: The costume note is missing the same part, as if someone deliberately left a blank.

zh_CN: 送行者是雨宫纱夜。
en: The sender was Amamiya Sayo.

zh_CN: 怪不得大人从来闭口不谈姐姐的名字，甚者说我是家中独女我从未有过姐姐
en: No wonder the adults never spoke my sister's name. They even said I was the only child in the family, as if I never had a sister at all.

zh_CN: 白狐回头时，童谣不让我叫的，都是这个名字。
en: When the white fox turns back, the nursery rhyme tells me not to call the name. It's always this name.

zh_CN: 五份记录对上了。
en: All five records match up.

zh_CN: 旧木额、回廊牌、衣箱、装束札和内殿档案，都缺一角，也都湿得发软。
en: The old plaque, corridor sign, chest, costume note, and inner archive all have a missing corner, and all are damp and soft.

zh_CN: 只差门口那块匾。
en: Only the plaque at the gate is left.

zh_CN: 如果狐火能照出旧字，也许能把缺角补上。
en: If foxfire can reveal the old characters, maybe the missing corner can be restored.

zh_CN: 匾额上的「送*之仪」被狐火照亮。
en: The 'Sending * Ritual' on the plaque is lit by foxfire.

zh_CN: 狐火贴近木面。剩余的金漆起泡，一点点自行剥落。
en: The foxfire presses close to the wood. The remaining gold paint blisters and peels away bit by bit on its own.

zh_CN: 遮住的字露了出来。
en: The hidden character is revealed.

zh_CN: 木匾变成了「送狐之仪」。
en: The wooden plaque becomes 'Sending the Fox Ritual'.

zh_CN: 既然木匾有了变化，我们去看看刚才箱子里的字到底是什么。
en: Since the plaque has changed, let's see exactly what the chest's missing word was.

zh_CN: 纸片上的遮挡松开了。
en: The covering on the paper loosens.

zh_CN: 刚才被框掉的字，是衣。
en: The character hidden in the box was clothing.

zh_CN: 我一路复原的是送行者的装束。
en: I've been reconstructing the sender's attire all along.

zh_CN: 再往内室走，白狐应该就在那里。
en: Go further into the inner chamber. The white fox should be there.

zh_CN: 白狐出现在长明灯前。灯下的石板渗着水。
en: The white fox appears before the eternal lamp. The stone slab beneath it is seeping water.

zh_CN: 它回过头。童谣里的禁句贴着耳边响起。
en: It turns back. The forbidden line from the nursery rhyme rings in your ears.

zh_CN: 纸门上的影子被灯火拉长，狐尾变成袖摆，耳尖变成发结。
en: The shadow on the paper door is stretched by the lantern light: the fox tail becomes sleeves, the ear tips become hair knots.

zh_CN: 长明灯照见一个穿白衣的孩子。
en: The eternal lamp reveals a child in white.

zh_CN: 雨宫……
en: Amamiya...

zh_CN: 白狐的耳朵动了一下。它听见了。
en: The white fox's ears twitch. It heard you.

zh_CN: 如果姐姐的全名被叫出来，狐位会松动。
en: If my sister's full name is spoken, the fox seat will loosen.

zh_CN: 但狐位一空，被压住的东西也会回到村里。
en: But if the fox seat empties, the things it suppresses will return to the village.
